name: new_can_reject_valid
reason: An always-Err implementation satisfies the spec vacuously, making bitmap creation dead code.

name: alloc_range_rejects_with_free
reason: Without a liveness guarantee linking free ranges to Ok, alloc_range can always fail, making the allocator useless.

name: set_err_can_mutate
reason: A missing frame condition on the Err path allows silent corruption of bitmap state on any failed set call.

name: clear_err_can_mutate
reason: Missing frame condition and undistinguished error cases let clear silently corrupt state and prevent callers from diagnosing failures.

name: alloc_always_returns_zero
reason: Without fairness, an implementation can starve all indices except 0, causing pathological fragmentation in the allocator.

name: alloc_range_empty_full_size_can_err
reason: An empty bitmap trivially has a free range of any valid size, so alloc_range should succeed but the spec permits Err

name: from_raw_array_no_err_branch
reason: The ensures has no Err branch, so the implementation's Err path is invisible to callers and there is no graceful degradation when preconditions are unverified

name: test_err_code_unspecified
reason: Callers that pattern-match on ErrorCode to distinguish out-of-bounds from other failures cannot rely on the spec for a consistent error type

name: new_no_ok_guarantee
reason: Without a success guarantee, verified callers cannot compose with new — they can exclude all Err conditions yet the spec still permits Err for any reason

name: set_err_codes_not_distinguished
reason: Error codes are part of the observable contract but the spec erases them, preventing callers from programmatically differentiating retry-able vs fatal errors

name: clear_no_alloc_hint
reason: Callers relying on temporal reuse (e.g., PID recycling) cannot reason about allocation order after free because the spec omits the next_free hint guarantee.

name: alloc_range_no_nextfit
reason: Sequential packing is important for fragmentation and cache locality; the spec permits arbitrary non-sequential allocation, breaking callers' determinism assumptions.

name: clear_err_codes_indistinguishable
reason: Without distinct error codes in the spec, callers cannot differentiate a bounds violation (programming error) from an already-cleared bit (logic error) for proper error handling.

name: set_no_hint_update
reason: Callers interleaving set and alloc cannot reason about allocation search order because the spec hides the asymmetric cursor behavior between set (no update) and alloc (advances cursor).

name: alloc_range_err_codes_ambiguous
reason: Without error-code discrimination in the spec, callers cannot decide whether to retry with a different size (InvalidArgument) or free resources first (OutOfMemory).
